relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Paul and Timothy speak from Divine will into the Colossian body.
Grace and peace open the letter.
Thanksgiving rises whenever they are remembered.
Trust in Messiah and love for the holy ones are heard.
Hope is stored in the heavens and announced by truth’s good news.
The good news bears fruit and grows wherever grace is truly known.
Epaphras served as trustworthy teacher of Messiah.
Their love is love in Spirit.
Prayer asks for wisdom-filled knowing of Divine will.
A worthy walk bears fruit and grows in knowing the Divine.
Glory-strength empowers endurance, patience, and joy.
The Father qualifies them for inheritance in light.
They are rescued from dark authority and transferred into the Son’s reign.
In the Beloved Son is release and forgiveness.
Messiah is image of the invisible Divine and firstborn of creation.
All visible and invisible powers are created in, through, and into him.
He is before all, and all things hold together in him.
He is head of the body and firstborn from the dead.
All fullness is pleased to dwell in him.
Through him all things are reconciled, peace made through the cross.
Alienated minds are brought into reconciliation.
His embodied death presents the community holy and unaccused.
Remain founded in the hope of the good news.
Paul’s suffering serves the body, the assembly.
His service fulfills the Divine word through stewardship.
The age-hidden mystery is now manifest.
The mystery among the nations is Messiah in you, hope of glory.
Messiah is proclaimed so every human may mature in him.
Paul labors by the power working within him.

contemplative_note: Colossians 1 is cosmic coherence: all things hold together in Messiah, fullness dwells in him, and the mystery is Messiah in you, hope of glory.
